elements.text: Grey ash cascades from a raw, trembling hand. Kaelen rolls onto his back, gasping as the freezing dawn air hits his lungs like crushed glass. Overhead, a bloated, copper sun struggles to pierce a heavy canopy of shifting soot.
Atmospheric density is roughly twelve percent higher than baseline. Particulate matter... carbon, sulphur, heavy metals. This body is inadequate, yet it functions.

He struggles to a sitting position. His limbs shake, thin and fragile beneath a coarse, dirt-caked tunic. A violent cough racks his chest, but his eyes remain sharp, cold, and intensely focused as he scans the desolate horizon.
Pulse is ninety-two. Muscular mass is depleted by approximately thirty percent. Yet, there is a secondary current... an ambient energy trying to occupy the cellular void.

He reaches down, his fingers brushing a jagged shard of obsidian half-buried in the ash. A faint, razor-sharp residue of energy hums against his skin. His analytical mind rapidly catalogs the sensation, aligning it with the brutal principles of this sword-cultivation world.
An efficient mechanism of self-directed evolution, hidden beneath a layer of barbaric mysticism. There are no safety margins here. No structural redundancy.
Kaelen forces himself to stand. He sways for a moment, then locks his knees, his posture hardening. He wipes the black soot from his lips and stares into the heart of the ash-choked wastes.
The rules of this system are rudimentary. I will master them.
